plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% as it is flagged for reduction.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Increase QQQ to 25% to favor broad index and quality names.
These moves reduce leverage and concentration, aligning with capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month outlook, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely and trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SMH to 8% and QQQI to 15% as they are flagged holdings.
Buy ZS and CRM at 5% each, adding quality tech from the scanner.
This balances growth with risk management, reducing overall leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while introducing new quality tech names, aiming for a moderate risk profile.

moves.note: Add inverse leveraged ETF for hedge

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely and trim SPAL to 3% to manage exposure in underperforming leveraged ETFs.
Trim SMH to 5% as it is flagged.
Buy GUSH at 10% and AAPL at 7% from the scanner's strongest setups.
Add SOXS at 5% as an inverse leveraged ETF, potentially hedging semis.
This aggressive plan leans into strong setups and leverage for higher potential growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, stretching for growth with increased leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher risk, including greater potential drawdown and daily decay/rebalancing effects of leveraged ETFs, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim CRWD to 5% and sell SMH to reduce high-beta and sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize income and monthly distributions.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth exposure.
This plan focuses on low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta exposure for lower drawdown, but distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
